rows.notes: Page title at top; text is well centered and clearly legible. Date 'Nov: 2. 1759' readable.

Pounds '28' and shillings '13' align clearly with vertical rules. Pence looks like a plain '5' (small loop present but consistent with 5d). Description legible though abbreviated; alignment with first numeric row is unambiguous.
Pounds '5' and shillings '18' are clear. Pence appears to be '4' though handwriting close to adjacent stroke; chosen '4' based on alignment with pence column.
Pounds '29' and shillings '16' clear. Pence looks like '4' but the loop is faint; moderate certainty.

Large amounts '46' and '16' are clear and well aligned. Pence appears to be '6' and there is an editorial note/word 'clear' nearby on the page which confirms this line as a well-ruled total for that item.
Pounds '9' and shillings '3' legible. Pence '4' readable though the descender of the previous line touches; chosen '4' due to column alignment.

rows.description: Little Ire. Suit Rents pro bien.
rows.notes: Pounds '6' and shillings '13' visible. Pence appears as '4' though slightly cramped; description is somewhat abbreviated but readable.

Pounds and shillings '6' and '13' align with the column, but the pence digit is a darker ink blot that could be '8' or '3'; chosen '8' based on stroke thickness and spacing relative to neighboring rows.

rows.description: Frethenven pro Anno et 4th Parte Anni
rows.notes: Pounds '7' and shillings '10' readable. Pence has a small loop suggesting '6' or possibly a halfpenny; there is a tiny flourish. Chosen whole pence '6' with moderate confidence.
Pounds '2' clear; shillings '0' appears as a small dash/space; pence looks like '4' aligned in pence column.
This line clearly shows 0 pounds, 2 shillings, 6 pence (small '0' in pounds column then '2' and '6' in their columns). Ink and alignment are clear.
Pounds '9' and shillings '13' clear. Pence '10' is two digits and sits squarely in the pence column; digits are legible though slightly compressed.
Pounds '4' and shillings '16' readable. Pence appears to be '7' (curved stroke). The description 'Molineus' is an abbreviation of 'Molineux' or 'Milling' but the amount alignment is reliable.
Pounds '4' and shillings '16' clear. Pence looks like a small '1' but ink faint—could be '1' or '7'; chosen '1' based on narrow stroke placement in pence column.
Pounds '4' and shillings '3' readable. Pence '4' visible; spacing between the three numeric columns matches other rows, supporting this reading.
Pounds column shows a small 0/dash; shillings '13' clear. Pence appears as '3' though slightly faded where the ink thins.
Digits '4', '12', '2' align with columns and are legible. Description hyphenation follows page script; numeric reading clear.
Pounds '1' clear. Shillings '9' and pence '3' readable though pence digit slightly faint; chosen based on consistent alignment with column lines.
Pounds '9' and shillings '6' readable. Pence appears to be '8' but the stroke is slightly smudged; alternative could be '0' or '3' but placement suggests '8'.
Pounds '8' visible though partially obscured by a fold shadow. Shillings '6' and pence '8' read by alignment; the pence digit has some ink bleed so confidence moderate.
Pounds '1' and shillings '3' readable; pence appears as '0' (a small dash/blank in pence column).

rows.description: Taster Newington Rect. pro An. et Dimid.
rows.notes: This line clearly shows a large round '80' in the pounds column and zeroes for shillings and pence. Numerals are large and unambiguous.
Pounds '40' is large and clear; shillings and pence show '0'. Alignment is unambiguous.
Pounds '2' and shillings '10' are clearly written. Pence shows as '0'. The name 'Shrup' / abbreviation is less clear but the amounts align well.
Pounds '4' and zeros for shillings/pence are clearly written and well-separated. Description legible.
Pounds '1' with zeros after; clear alignment and minimal ambiguity.
Pounds column shows '0' or dash; shillings look like '10' but the first digit is faint. Chosen '10' based on spacing and comparison to other '10' entries on page.
Pounds '1' clear. Shillings look like '0' (small dash). Pence appears as '4' but the stroke is faint; moderate confidence.
Pounds '1' and shillings '13' clear. Pence '4' visible; description readable though small flourish on the hyphen.
Pounds column blank/dash; shillings appear to be '10' but first digit faint. Pence blank/zero. Description clear; numeric certainty moderate.

rows.description: Mledenham pro Bien et 3d. Partis Anni
rows.notes: Pounds '10' and shillings '6' readable though text of the place name is cramped and partly obscured by a small stain; pence shows '0'.

This line is heavily struck through with a long ink stroke; the name (Guinear or similar) is partly visible but the whole row has been cancelled and no amount is legible. Low confidence due to strike-through and uncertain spelling.

rows.description: Ennuis Back-gate pro Anno et Dimid.
rows.notes: Very large, clear '175' in pounds column; shillings and pence are '0'. Numerals are prominent and unambiguous.

rows.description: Wild Arish pro Bien et 4th Parte Anni
rows.notes: Pounds look like '13' and shillings '10' but the handwriting of the pounds could possibly be '11' or '13' (first digit slightly squeezed). Chosen '13' based on stroke thickness and comparison to preceding '175' and '80' numerals.

rows.description: D^o A Bond from Gooch w. Interest
rows.notes: Final line shows a clearly written '14' in the pounds column and '00/00' style zeros for shillings and pence; description referencing a bond and interest is legible.
